steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: On the left is a noun phrase—“such symbiotic relationships” with a modifying relative clause. On the right is a noun phrase—“extensive networks” with a modifying relative clause. A finite verb is needed to connect them as the main predicate.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule
steps.content:
A semicolon joins two independent clauses, so the second must have a finite verb as its main predicate. “Form” is simple present tense and agrees with “relationships.” The other options—forming, to form, and having formed—are non-finite forms that cannot serve as an independent clause's main verb.

That confirms D is correct.
